basics.name: Sydney Hashiro
basics.label: Computer Science Student | IT Support & Analyst
basics.summary: Computer Science student specializing in cybersecurity and full-stack development, with hands-on experience troubleshooting technology and building web applications using React, Next.js, and SQL-based databases. Experienced in customer-facing technical support and problem-solving in fast-paced environments. Seeking an entry-level IT support or analyst role starting Summer 2026.

work.summary: Execute merchandising strategies and maintain operational efficiency across departments while collaborating with leadership to improve workflow execution.
highlights: Executed planograms and merchandising strategies to support operational efficiency
Collaborated with leadership to prioritize workload and improve workflow execution
Identified inefficiencies and improved organization across departments
Trained and supported team members to improve performance and adaptability
Maintained inventory accuracy and ensured product availability

work.summary: Provided customer-facing technical support for electronics, mobile devices, and consumer technology products.
highlights: Assisted customers in troubleshooting device issues and understanding product functionality
Provided technical support for electronics, mobile devices, and consumer technology
Communicated technical concepts clearly to non-technical users
Recommended technology solutions based on customer needs
Supported operations including POS systems and inventory management

work.summary: Supervised swimmers, enforced safety protocols, and responded to emergencies while teaching swim lessons and CPR courses.
highlights: Supervised swimmers and enforced safety protocols
Responded to emergencies using CPR and lifesaving techniques
Taught swim lessons and CPR certification courses

projects.name: Da Grindz — Full-Stack Web App
projects.summary: Developed a full-stack meal planning application using React, Next.js, TypeScript, Prisma, and PostgreSQL.
highlights: Implemented drag-and-drop functionality, macro tracking, and database persistence
Designed API routes and dynamic UI components
Developed using VS Code and Git version control
Integrated backend data handling for dynamic user interaction

projects.name: Systems Programming (ICS 212)
projects.summary: Built and compiled C programs in a Unix/Linux environment.
highlights: Compiled programs using GCC in a terminal environment
Used Makefiles for build automation
Worked with file I/O and debugging in command-line environments
